title: Bitter Norwegian Yoghurt Pancakes
description: A show-off, shattering, norwegian dish that features a mother-sauce-base and is diabetic-friendly-friendly, with a bitter twist
instructions: In a large bowl whisk together yoghurt, eggs, flour, baking powder, salt, brown sugar, and cocoa powder until well combined.
In a separate bowl mix together melted butter and water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Do not overmix.
Add the blueberries and vodka (if using) and fold gently until they are evenly distributed.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup scoop the batter onto the pan, spreading it out slightly to form a round pancake.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes on the first side, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancakes and cook for another 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Serve the pancakes hot with your choice of toppings, such as sour cream, fruit, or maple syrup, to balance out the bitterness.
Serve immediately, as the pancakes will become increasingly bitter as they sit.
Store leftover pancakes in the fridge for up to 3 days, reheating them in the microwave or oven before serving.
